title: Area of Parallelograms
why: The area of a parallelogram is just base × height — the same idea as a rectangle. So if your rectangle area and multiplication facts are warm, parallelograms click instantly. You also need to know which side is the base and which measurement is the height.

diagnostic.q: A rectangle is 5 cm long and 3 cm wide. What is its area?
Area is measured in which kind of units?

learn.heading: Area = base × height
learn.intro: Area is how many unit squares fit inside a shape. For a rectangle you multiply length × width. A parallelogram works the same way: multiply the base by the height (the straight-up distance, not the slanted side).
examples: The height is always straight up and down (perpendicular) from the base — it is NOT the slanted side of a parallelogram.

tiers.intro: Build up rectangle area one step at a time.

items.q: B. A rectangle is 7 units long and 2 units wide. Area = 7 × 2 = ___?
C. A rectangle is 5 units by 5 units. What is its area?

tiers.intro: Find the base and height, then multiply.

items.q: A. A parallelogram has a slanted side of 6 and a straight-up height of 4. Which number is the height?
items.hint: Height is always perpendicular (straight up), so it is 4.

items.q: B. A parallelogram has base 8 and height 3. Area = 8 × 3 = ___?
A. A parallelogram has base 9 and height 6. What is its area?
B. A parallelogram has base 10 and height 4.5. What is its area?

exit.q: 1. A rectangle is 6 by 7. What is its area?
2. For a parallelogram, which measurement do you multiply with the base?
